message:
Add dependency injection for WebSocket in useGraphSubscription tests

Problem:
- Tests mutated globalThis.WebSocket to inject mock
- Causes race conditions when tests run concurrently (parallel test files)
- Required careful beforeEach/afterEach cleanup of global state

Solution:
- Add __testCreateWebSocket option for dependency injection
- Add wsUrl option to avoid window.location dependency in tests
- Tests pass WebSocket factory via props instead of mutating globals
- Remove afterEach cleanup (no globals to restore)

Hook changes (useCollabGraph.ts):
- Add wsUrl?: string option for WebSocket URL override
- Add __testCreateWebSocket?: (url: string) => WebSocket for test DI
- Default wsUrl builds from window.location (existing behavior)
- Wrap URL parsing in try/catch - invalid URLs set connected=false
- Use factory function when provided, fall back to new WebSocket()
- Add new options to useEffect dependency array

Test changes (useCollabGraphSubscription.test.tsx):
- Remove global WebSocket/location mocking in beforeEach/afterEach
- Remove afterEach hook entirely (no cleanup needed)
- SubscriptionProbe passes wsUrl and __testCreateWebSocket
- createWebSocket factory returns MockWebSocket instance
- Tests are now isolated and safe for parallel execution

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
